meta.operator: City of Winnipeg Water and Waste Department
meta.per_capita_lpd_source: City of Winnipeg 2023 Water Quality Report
data_licences: Open Government Licence – Canada (Environment and Climate Change Canada)

source.primary: Shoal Lake (Lake of the Woods), Ontario/Manitoba border — conveyed 154km via Greater Winnipeg Water District Aqueduct
source.wfi_note: Boreal Shield source watershed; low wildfire transmission risk to intake

shoal_lake.name: Shoal Lake (natural lake)
shoal_lake.data_note: Shoal Lake is a large natural lake — capacity effectively unlimited relative to Winnipeg's demand. Real-time levels not in WSC network.

storage.data_note: Shoal Lake provides effectively unlimited storage. The constraint is aqueduct capacity (390 ML/day), not lake volume.

plants.name: Deacon WTP
plants.process: UV, chlorination, fluoride, orthophosphate (corrosion control), opened 2009

treatment.data_note: The 154km gravity-fed aqueduct from Shoal Lake was built in 1919. Deacon WTP (2009) replaced the original treatment infrastructure. Orthophosphate added for lead pipe corrosion control.

restriction.hypothesized_triggers: Winnipeg has a seasonal odd/even outdoor watering bylaw. Full restrictions uncommon given large source volume.

snowpack.reference: April 1 peak, Lake of the Woods Basin

risk.single_point_of_failure: The Greater Winnipeg Water District Aqueduct (154km, built 1919) is the sole conveyance from Shoal Lake. A major aqueduct failure would cut supply with no immediate alternative.
risk.climate_note: Source water security is excellent. The primary risk is the aging aqueduct and the justice implications of the Shoal Lake #40 First Nation situation.

use.data_note: Sector split approximate from City of Winnipeg annual report

plants: North End Water Pollution Control Centre
South End Water Pollution Control Centre
egress.cso_note: Winnipeg has combined sewers in older neighbourhoods. North End WPCC upgrades ongoing to reduce CSO events.

red_emerson.station_name: Red River at Emerson (US border crossing)
